mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid labor market deterioration signals an imminent economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal exhibits no predictive power over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and residential housing starts confirms broad-based economic expansion, reinforcing corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in forward 20-day returns during confirmed expansion phases, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a supply-constrained environment indicates successful capacity utilization and pricing power, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain flat or negative when industrial production exceeds its 12-month moving average, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent federal funds rates above 4.5% maintain restrictive financial conditions that compress equity multiples and suppress forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline when the funds rate exceeds 4.5%, or the signal generates positive returns in more than 40% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in employment payrolls and consumer credit outstanding confirms robust household spending capacity, triggering immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: The combined payroll and credit signal does not produce statistically significant positive 5-day forward returns, or the interaction effect reverses sign during high-volatility market regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic momentum that supports corporate earnings revisions and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are not significantly higher than the historical median when GDP growth remains positive, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.5 over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated unemployment rates during a post-shock recovery phase indicate structural labor market friction that drags on consumer demand and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive or neutral when unemployment exceeds 4.0% during recovery periods, or the signal exhibits no directional consistency across multiple recovery episodes.
